{
  "claims": [
    "Mental health needs have accounted for the largest share of hospital admissions of children ages 0 to 17 in California since 2008.",
    "California is not prepared to care for the increasing number of pediatric mental health crises.",
    "Families often wait days or weeks in a hospital without mental health services for their children during crises.",
    "The Annie E. Casey Foundation reports that California's children experienced the second largest increase in anxiety and depression in the country from 2016 to 2020.",
    "COVID-19 has exacerbated the increase in anxiety and depression among children in California.",
    "Approximately 20 percent of youth between ages 13 and 18 live with a mental health condition according to the National Alliance on Mental Illness.",
    "An estimated three out of four children in the United States do not receive the mental health services they need.",
    "Suicide is the second leading cause of death among adolescents."
  ]
